data.title: Former HKEX CEO: Tokenization cannot reduce the risks of underlying real-world assets.
data.description: Li Xiaojia, former CEO of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and co-founder and chairman of DripNet, responded to the question of whether DripNet, after launching its private equity fund process, might follow the new regulatory rules issued by the China Securities Regulatory Commission in February this year to be the first in the market to "produce" the tokenization of real-world assets (RWA). He stated that DripNet currently has no need for RWA tokenization and there is no need to issue RWA tokens, as tokenization cannot reduce the risk of the underlying real-world assets. Li Xiaojia also divided virtual asset investors into five categories: the first category is the earliest entrants, the second category is mid-term entrants, the third category is traditional financial institutions, the fourth category is traditional financial professionals with decentralized concepts, and the fifth category is what people commonly refer to as "leeks" (new investors who are easily exploited). (Caixin.com)

data.title: Analysis: US crude oil futures break $85 for the first time in nearly two years, disrupting the global energy "lifeline".
data.description: US crude oil futures broke through $85 a barrel for the first time in nearly two years, with the Middle East war triggering severe turmoil in the energy market and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz almost completely halted. With no signs of abating, Goldman Sachs warned that oil prices risk breaking $100 if supply disruptions continue; European diesel futures rose by more than 50% in a single week; and central banks around the world expressed unease about a possible resurgence of inflation. Qatar's energy minister even warned that oil prices could reach $150. Faced with importers' difficulties in obtaining crude oil, the US Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control issued a short-term exemption, allowing India to purchase Russian crude oil. As the escalating conflict restricted Middle East supplies, Saudi Arabia raised its April prices for its main crude oil products sold to Asia by the largest margin since August 2022. Riyadh is also diverting millions of barrels of crude oil to its Red Sea ports to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 70,000 USDT with a 3.97% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 06, 2026, 12:08 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 70,000 USDT and is now trading at 69,993.648438 USDT, with a narrowed 3.97% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: EU Considers Financial Support to Restore 'Friendship' Pipeline Supply
data.description: The European Union Commission is evaluating potential financial assistance to aid in the restoration of supply through the 'Friendship' oil pipeline. According to Jin10, this move comes as part of efforts to stabilize energy supplies in the region. The pipeline, which plays a crucial role in transporting oil, has faced disruptions, prompting the EU to explore measures to ensure a steady flow of resources. The Commission's consideration of financial support underscores the importance of the pipeline in maintaining energy security and economic stability within the EU.

data.title: BlackRock deposited 2,750 BTC, worth over $190 million, into a Coinbase Prime address.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, BlackRock has just transferred a total of 2,750 BTC, worth approximately $194.04 million, to a Coinbase Prime address through its Bitcoin exchange-traded fund IBIT. Further transactions may follow.

data.title: Turkey Suspends Flights to Middle Eastern Countries Until March 9
data.description: Turkey's Minister of Transport has announced the suspension of flights to and from Iran, Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, and Jordan until March 9. According to Jin10, this decision affects all flights between Turkey and these Middle Eastern countries. The suspension is part of Turkey's broader strategy to address regional concerns and ensure safety in air travel. The move comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and aims to mitigate risks associated with travel in the region. Passengers are advised to check with airlines for updates on flight schedules and alternative arrangements.

data.title: Bit Digital disclosed that its ETH holdings increased to 155,434.4 coins at the end of February, worth over $305 million.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury company Bit Digital released its holdings update report, showing that as of February 28, 2026, its Ethereum holdings increased to 155,434.4 ETH, worth approximately $305.4 million, with an average purchase price of $3,045. Furthermore, the company disclosed that as of the end of February, it had staked 1,382,696 ETH, representing approximately 89% of its total holdings, generating approximately 313.9 ETH in staking rewards. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Expert: Euro Falls to Four-Week Low Against Pound Amid Energy Price Concerns
data.description: Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown has highlighted the recent decline of the euro against the pound, reaching a four-week low. According to Jin10, this movement is attributed to market perceptions that the eurozone is more vulnerable to energy price shocks compared to the UK. Brown noted that since the onset of the conflict last weekend, market participants have largely viewed developments in the Middle East from this perspective. Additionally, he mentioned that the market has almost fully priced in the expectation of an interest rate hike by the European Central Bank by the end of the year. This anticipation could further pressure the euro, as it may lead to market discounting of growth challenges.

data.title: 10x Research: Mining companies' transformation into AI infrastructure may reshape the next cycle and the structure of the crypto stock market.
data.description: A recent analysis released by 10x Research on its X platform indicates that institutional inflows and changes in corporate balance sheets are quietly reshaping the cryptocurrency stock market landscape, driving up the prices of some crypto-related stocks. Circle's stock price has rebounded by approximately 60%, and Coinbase has risen by about 25%. Meanwhile, the transformation of mining companies into AI infrastructure may reshape the next cycle. Bitdeer previously liquidated its Bitcoin reserves. Although its equity was diluted after completing the convertible bond issuance and stock placement, the proceeds may be used to accelerate its transformation towards high-performance computing and AI infrastructure. This means that the cryptocurrency mining sector is also undergoing a structural shift, potentially redefining investors' judgments on the next cycle.

data.title: Bitwise donated $100,000 to two Ethereum open-source development organizations.
data.description: Crypto asset management firm Bitwise Asset Management announced its second donation of $100,000 to Ethereum open-source developers, awarded to two Ethereum open-source developer organizations, Protocol Guild and the PBS Foundation. Bitwise previously pledged to donate 10% of the annual returns from its Bitwise Ethereum exchange-traded fund (ETHW) to support the development of the Bitcoin network; the ETF has to date attracted over $361 million in inflows. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Rapper-Turned-Politician's Party Leads in Nepal Elections
data.description: A political party led by a 35-year-old rapper-turned-politician is currently leading in Nepal's national elections. Bloomberg posted on X that this development signals a potential shift in the country's political landscape, challenging the long-standing dominance of traditional political parties in the Himalayan nation. The party's unexpected rise reflects a growing desire for change among the electorate, who are seeking alternatives to the established political order. As the election results continue to unfold, the implications for Nepal's future governance remain significant.

data.title: Global Central Banks Shift Gold Holdings Amid Strategic Changes
data.description:
Recent developments indicate a shift in the gold holdings of several global central banks. According to Jin10, Poland has announced plans to sell gold in order to finance the purchase of tanks, marking a significant strategic move. Meanwhile, Venezuela is reportedly conducting an emergency sale of its gold reserves, and Russia has quietly reduced its gold holdings. These actions suggest a break in the long-standing trend of central banks predominantly buying rather than selling gold.

The reasons behind these changes are not entirely clear, raising questions about whether gold has lost its appeal or if there are underlying strategic motives. Observers are keenly watching to see which country might be next to adjust its gold reserves. This shift could have implications for global gold markets and central bank strategies moving forward.

data.title: WTI crude oil has returned above the $85 mark.
data.description: WTI crude oil touched the $85/barrel mark for the first time since April 2024, surging 7.7% on the day. Brent crude oil rose 5% on the day, currently trading at $86.53/barrel. (Jinshi)

data.title: Payments technology company Silverflow has raised $40 million in Series B funding, led by Picus Capital.
data.description: Payment technology company Silverflow announced the completion of a $40 million Series B funding round, led by Picus Capital, with participation from Rabobank's investment arm Rabo Investments, Inkef, Crane, Coatue, and GPT. Its platform provides cloud-native payment processing solutions, supporting features such as tokenized payments and bank card payments. (Fintechfutures)

data.title: Consumer Discretionary Companies Face Weakest Earnings in Six Years
data.description: Large-cap consumer discretionary companies have reported their weakest earnings season in nearly six years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that high fixed costs, reduced demand, and persistently elevated prices have significantly impacted their financial results. The sector, which includes businesses such as retail and entertainment, has struggled to maintain profitability amid these challenging conditions. Analysts suggest that the combination of these factors has created a difficult environment for growth, with companies needing to adapt to changing consumer behaviors and economic pressures. As the industry navigates these hurdles, stakeholders are closely monitoring how these companies will strategize to improve their performance in the coming quarters.

data.title: Superpower Completes Pre-Seed Funding Round
data.description: AI agent revenue protocol Superpower has announced the completion of its pre-seed funding round on the X platform. According to ChainCatcher, the amount raised has not been disclosed. The funding round saw participation from Taisu Ventures, 280 Capital, Paper Ventures, and Catcher VC.

data.title: US media: Russia provides Iran with location data for US troops, indirectly intervening in the Middle East conflict.
data.description: According to the Washington Post, three officials familiar with intelligence revealed that Russia is providing Iran with location information to attack U.S. forces in the Middle East. This is the first indication that another major U.S. adversary is involved in the war, even if only indirectly. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Reinsurers Issue Cancellation Notices Following US-Iran Naval Incident
data.description: London-based reinsurers have begun issuing seven-day cancellation notices on marine war-risk coverage. Bloomberg posted on X that this action follows a recent incident where a US submarine torpedoed an Iranian warship near Sri Lanka. The move reflects heightened tensions and potential risks in maritime operations in the region. The cancellation notices are expected to impact shipping companies operating in affected areas, prompting them to reassess their insurance needs and risk management strategies. The situation underscores the ongoing geopolitical challenges and their implications for global maritime insurance markets.

data.title: Germany to Decide on Future Combat Air System Project in Coming Weeks
data.description: Germany's government spokesperson announced that a decision regarding the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) project will be made in the coming weeks. According to Jin10, this development is part of ongoing discussions about the future of European defense capabilities. The FCAS project, a collaborative effort between Germany, France, and Spain, aims to develop a next-generation fighter jet and associated systems to enhance military capabilities. The decision is expected to address various aspects of the project, including funding, technological development, and international cooperation. The outcome will have significant implications for the participating countries and the broader European defense landscape.

data.title: Roblox Teen Creators Earn Significant Income Amid Industry Challenges
data.description: Teen creators on Roblox are generating substantial monthly earnings from simple games, despite major studios facing job cuts and project cancellations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the contrast between the success of these young developers and the struggles of larger gaming companies. The platform's user-friendly tools and vast audience have enabled these teenagers to monetize their creations effectively. As the gaming industry undergoes significant changes, Roblox continues to provide opportunities for young talent to thrive financially.
